info.description:
# 🚀 Hypris: Next-Generation Workflow Management Platform

## Executive Summary

**Hypris** is a cutting-edge, enterprise-grade workflow management system that revolutionizes how teams organize, visualize, and collaborate on their work. Built on a flexible, API-first architecture, Hypris combines the power of customizable databases, real-time collaboration tools, and intelligent data visualization to create a unified workspace where teams can manage any workflow—from project management and CRM to product development and operations.

Unlike traditional workflow tools that force you into rigid structures, Hypris adapts to your needs with **23+ field types**, **5 visualization modes**, and a hierarchical organization system that scales from small teams to enterprise deployments. Whether you're tracking customer relationships, managing complex projects, or building custom business applications, Hypris provides the flexibility and power to make it happen.

---

## 🎯 Key Features & Capabilities

### **Flexible Data Architecture**

- **Custom Databases**: Create unlimited databases with fully customizable schemas
    
- **23+ Property Types**: From simple text fields to advanced formulas and relations
    
- **Dynamic Views**: Visualize the same data in 5 different ways simultaneously
    
- **Smart Filtering**: Build complex filter groups to surface exactly what you need
    

### **Real-Time Collaboration**

- **Conversations**: Threaded discussions attached to any resource
    
- **Calls**: Integrated video/audio calling within your workspace
    
- **Comments**: Contextual feedback on any item or database entry
    
- **People Fields**: Assign team members and track ownership
    

### **Advanced Capabilities**

- **Formula Engine**: Create calculated fields with custom logic
    
- **Relational Data**: Link databases together with bi-directional relations
    
- **Time Tracking**: Built-in time tracker for productivity monitoring
    
- **Auto-numbering**: Automatic ID generation for tracking and reference
    
- **Teleport Fields**: Quick navigation between related resources
    

### **Enterprise-Ready**

- **Multi-Workspace Support**: Separate environments for different teams or clients
    
- **Hierarchical Organization**: Workspaces → Spaces → Resources structure
    
- **Robust REST API**: Full programmatic access to all features
    
- **Comprehensive CRUD Operations**: Complete control over every entity
    

## 💼 Use Cases

Hypris adapts to virtually any workflow. Here are some powerful examples:

### **Project Management** 🎯

- **Databases**: Projects, Tasks, Milestones, Resources
    
- **Views**: Kanban for workflow, Timeline for scheduling, Calendar for deadlines
    
- **Properties**: Status, Assignee, Priority, Due Date, Time Tracker
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for feedback, Calls for standups
    

### **Customer Relationship Management (CRM)** 🤝

- **Databases**: Contacts, Companies, Deals, Activities
    
- **Views**: Kanban for sales pipeline, Map for territory management, Table for data entry
    
- **Properties**: Status, Contact Info (Phone, Email), Location, Relations (Company → Contacts)
    
- **Collaboration**: Conversations for account planning, Comments for deal notes
    

### **Product Development** 🚀

- **Databases**: Features, Bugs, Releases, User Feedback
    
- **Views**: Kanban for sprint planning, Timeline for release planning, Table for backlog grooming
    
- **Properties**: Status, Priority, Assignee, Story Points, Relations (Feature → Tasks)
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for technical discussions, Files for design specs
    

### **Content Management** ✍️

- **Databases**: Articles, Authors, Publications, Editorial Calendar
    
- **Views**: Calendar for publishing schedule, Kanban for editorial workflow, Table for content inventory
    
- **Properties**: Status, Author (People), Publish Date, Categories (Dropdown), SEO Score (Rating)
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for editorial feedback, Rich Text for content drafts
    

### **Event Planning** 🎉

- **Databases**: Events, Venues, Vendors, Attendees
    
- **Views**: Calendar for event schedule, Map for venue locations, Table for vendor management
    
- **Properties**: Date, Location, Status, Budget (Number), Checklist for tasks
    
- **Collaboration**: Conversations for planning, Files for contracts
    

### **Human Resources** 👔

- **Databases**: Employees, Positions, Candidates, Performance Reviews
    
- **Views**: Table for employee directory, Kanban for hiring pipeline, Calendar for review cycles
    
- **Properties**: People, Department (Dropdown), Start Date, Rating, Files (resumes, documents)
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for feedback, Conversations for team discussions
    

### **Real Estate** 🏠

- **Databases**: Properties, Clients, Showings, Transactions
    
- **Views**: Map for property locations, Kanban for deal stages, Calendar for showings
    
- **Properties**: Location, Price (Number), Status, Photos (Files), Agent (People)
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for property notes, Calls for client communication
    

### **Manufacturing & Operations** 🏭

- **Databases**: Orders, Inventory, Suppliers, Production Schedule
    
- **Views**: Timeline for production planning, Table for inventory management, Kanban for order fulfillment
    
- **Properties**: Status, Quantity (Number), Auto ID for order numbers, Relations (Order → Items)
    
- **Collaboration**: Comments for quality issues, Time Tracker for production time
    

---

## 🔌 Integration & API

Hypris is built API-first, providing complete programmatic access to all features:

### **RESTful Architecture**

Clean, predictable REST API with standard HTTP methods:

- `GET` - Retrieve resources
    
- `POST` - Create resources
    
- `PATCH` - Update resources
    
- `PUT` - Replace resources
    
- `DELETE` - Remove resources
    

### **Comprehensive Coverage**

Every feature accessible via API:

- Workspace management
    
- Space organization
    
- Resource creation and management
    
- Database schema definition
    
- Item CRUD operations
    
- View configuration
    
- Property management
    
- Filter creation
    

## 🌟 Why Choose Hypris?

### **Flexibility Without Complexity**

Hypris provides enterprise-grade power without the enterprise-grade complexity. The intuitive structure and comprehensive API make it accessible to both end-users and developers.

### **Single Source of Truth**

Consolidate your workflows, data, and collaboration into one platform. No more context switching between project management, CRM, spreadsheets, and communication tools.

### **Scales With You**

From solo entrepreneurs to enterprise teams, Hypris grows with your needs:

- Start simple with basic databases
    
- Add complexity as needed with relations and formulas
    
- Scale to multiple workspaces and teams
    
- Integrate with your existing tools via API
    

### **Visual Flexibility**

The same data, five different views. Let each team member work in their preferred format while maintaining a single source of truth.

### **Collaboration Built-In**

Communication isn't an afterthought—it's integrated into every aspect of Hypris. Conversations, Calls, and Comments keep teams aligned without leaving the platform.

### **Future-Proof**

The robust API ensures Hypris can adapt to your changing needs. Build custom integrations, automate workflows, and extend functionality as your requirements evolve.

**Hypris** represents the next evolution in workflow management—a platform that combines the flexibility of spreadsheets, the power of databases, the collaboration of modern communication tools, and the extensibility of an API-first architecture.

Whether you're managing projects, tracking customers, planning events, or building custom business applications, Hypris provides the foundation to work smarter, collaborate better, and scale effortlessly.

**Welcome to the future of work. Welcome to Hypris.** 🚀

## Add Item to Database

This endpoint allows users to add a new item to a specified database. The request is sent via an HTTP POST method to the designated URL, which incorporates the unique identifier for the database.

### Request Body

The request body must be formatted as a JSON object and includes the following parameters:

- **state** (string): Represents the current state of the item. For this request, the value should be set to "published".
    
- **cellValues** (object): This is an object that holds key-value pairs representing the values for each cell in the item. Currently, it is an empty object.
    
- **filter** (any): This parameter is used to apply any filters to the item. It can be set to `null` if no filters are needed.
    
- **search** (any): This parameter allows for searching within the item. It can also be set to `null` if no search criteria are specified.
    
- **id** (any): This is an optional parameter that can be used to specify an identifier for the item. It can be set to `null` if no specific ID is assigned.
    

### Expected Response

Upon successful execution of the request, the API will return a response that confirms the addition of the item to the database. The structure of the response typically includes:

- A confirmation message indicating the success of the operation.
    
- The ID of the newly created item.
    
- Any additional metadata related to the item that was added.
    

Ensure that the request body adheres to the specified format for successful processing.

# Get Database Properties

## Overview
This endpoint fetches a list of all properties (columns) associated with a specific database ID. It includes metadata such as property titles, data types, and unique identifiers.

## URL Parameters

### Path Parameters
- **database_id** (required): The unique identifier of the database whose properties you want to retrieve
  - Example: `{{database_id_baza1}}`
  - Format: String/UUID

### Query Parameters
- **includeDrafts** (optional): Controls whether draft properties should be included in the response
  - Type: Boolean
  - Values: `true` or `false`
  - Default: `false` (if not specified)
  - When `true`: Returns both published and draft properties
  - When `false`: Returns only published/active properties

## Expected Response Format

The endpoint returns a JSON object with a success flag and a data object containing an array of property objects:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "properties": [
      {
        "id": "697b5d66409d3115e489fe36",
        "title": "Name",
        "type": "text",
        "databaseId": "697b5d66409d3115e489fe32",
        "width": 200,
        "position": 0,
        "isPinned": true,
        "state": "published"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Response Fields
- **id**: Unique identifier for the property
- **title**: Display name of the property/column
- **type**: Data type (text, number, date, dropdown, etc.)
- **databaseId**: ID of the parent database
- **width**: Column width in pixels
- **position**: Order position of the property
- **isPinned**: Whether the property is pinned in views
- **state**: Publication state (published/draft)

## Common Use Cases

1. **Database Schema Discovery**: Retrieve all available columns to understand the database structure
2. **Dynamic Form Generation**: Fetch properties to dynamically build input forms for creating/editing records
3. **Data Validation**: Get property types and constraints to validate data before submission
4. **Property Management**: List all properties including drafts when managing database configuration
5. **Integration Setup**: Discover available fields when setting up integrations or data synchronization

## Notes About includeDrafts Parameter

- **Draft properties** are properties that have been created or modified but not yet published
- Use `includeDrafts=true` when:
  - Building administrative interfaces that need to show unpublished changes
  - Testing new property configurations before they go live
  - Reviewing pending changes to the database schema
- Use `includeDrafts=false` (or omit the parameter) when:
  - Building end-user interfaces that should only show active fields
  - Querying production data structures
  - Ensuring consistency with the published database schema

## Performance Notes
- This endpoint returns metadata only (property definitions), not the actual data stored in those properties
- Response time is typically fast as it's retrieving configuration data
- Consider caching the response if property definitions don't change frequently

### Update Property Title

This endpoint allows you to update the title of a specific property identified by its unique `newlyCreatedColumnId`. By sending a PATCH request to this endpoint, you can modify the existing title of the property.

#### Request

- **Method**: PATCH
    
- **URL**: `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedColumnId}}`
    

#### Request Body

The request body must be sent in JSON format and should contain the following key:

- **title** (string): The new title for the property.
    

**Example Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "title": "Auto ID1"
}

 ```

#### Response

Upon a successful update, the response will typically include the updated property details, confirming the change. The structure of the response will include:

- **id** (string): The unique identifier of the property.
    
- **title** (string): The updated title of the property.
    
- **updatedAt** (string): Timestamp indicating when the property was last updated.
    

**Example Response:**

``` json
{
  "id": "12345",
  "title": "Auto ID1",
  "updatedAt": "2023-10-01T12:00:00Z"
}

 ```

Ensure that the `newlyCreatedColumnId` is valid and corresponds to an existing property to avoid errors during the update process.

request.description:
# Update Property Text

## Overview
This endpoint updates the text property value for a specific item in your Hypris workspace. It allows you to modify text-type properties by sending the new value in the request body.

## Required Variables

This request uses the following variables that must be set before execution:

| Variable | Description | Source |
|----------|-------------|--------|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base API URL for your Hypris instance | Environment variable (DEV/WEB) |
| `{{current_item_id}}` | The unique identifier of the item to update | Set by running GET/POST Get Items request first |
| `{{prop_text}}` | The property ID of the text field to update | Set by running GET/POST Get Items request first |

## Request Body Structure

The request body uses a `cellValues` object where the key is the property ID and the value is the new text content:

```json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_text}}": "Updated text value"
  }
}
```

- **cellValues**: Object containing property ID to value mappings
- **{{prop_text}}**: Dynamic property ID that resolves to the actual text property identifier
- **Value**: The new text string to set for this property

## Prerequisites

⚠️ **Important**: Before running this request, you must first execute the **GET/POST Get Items** request to populate the required variables:
- `current_item_id`
- `prop_text`

Without these variables, the pre-request script will prevent the request from being sent.

## Pre-request Script Functionality

The pre-request script performs validation before sending the request:

1. **Retrieves Variables**: Fetches `current_item_id` and `prop_text` from collection variables
2. **Logs Debug Information**: Outputs the Item ID and Property ID to the console for verification
3. **Validation Check**: Throws an error if either variable is missing, preventing invalid API calls

This ensures you don't accidentally send requests with undefined values.

## Expected Response

### Success Response (200 OK)

```json
{
  "success": true
}
```

The post-response script automatically validates:
- ✅ Status code is 200
- ✅ Response JSON contains `success: true`

### Error Responses

If the request fails, the console will display an error message with the returned status code.

## Post-response Script Functionality

The test script performs the following checks:

1. **Status Code Validation**: Confirms the response status is 200
2. **Success Confirmation**: Verifies the response JSON contains a `success` field set to `true`
3. **Error Logging**: If the status is not 200, logs the error code to the console

## Notes

- This request is part of the property update workflow and follows the same pattern as other property update requests (Number, Rich Text, Link, etc.)
- The property ID (`prop_text`) is dynamically resolved, allowing you to update different text properties by changing the variable value
- All validation happens automatically through the scripts, providing immediate feedback if something is misconfigured

## DELETE Property Endpoint

This endpoint allows users to delete a specific property identified by its unique ID.

### Request

- **Method**: DELETE
    
- **URL**: `{{base_url}}//property/{{newlyCreatedTextPropertyId}}`
    

### Request Parameters

- **newlyCreatedTextPropertyId** (Path Parameter):
    
    - **Type**: String
        
    - **Description**: The unique identifier of the property that you wish to delete.
        

### Expected Response

Upon successful deletion, the API will return a response indicating the status of the operation. The typical response includes:

- **Status Code**: 204 No Content
    
    - Indicates that the request was successful and the property has been deleted.
        

### Notes

- Ensure that the property ID provided in the request URL is valid and corresponds to an existing property.
    
- Deleting a property is irreversible; once deleted, the property cannot be recovered.
    
- The request does not require a body; the example payload provided is not necessary for the DELETE operation.
    

Use this endpoint with caution, as it permanently removes the specified property from the system.

## Add Property to Database

This endpoint allows users to add a new property to a specified database. It is designed to accept a JSON payload containing details about the property being added.

# Update Property Number

## Overview
This endpoint updates a number property value for a specific item in your Hypris workspace. It allows you to modify numeric cell values by providing the property ID and the new numeric value.

## Endpoint Details

**Method:** `PATCH`

**URL Structure:** `{{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values`

### Path Variables
- `{{base_url}}` - The base URL for the Hypris API (set in environment variables)
- `{{current_item_id}}` - The unique identifier of the item whose property you want to update (typically set by a previous "Get Items" request)

## Request Body

The request body uses a `cellValues` object where the key is the property ID and the value is the new number to set:

```json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_number}}": 42.5
  }
}
```

### Body Parameters
- `cellValues` (object) - Container for property updates
  - `{{prop_number}}` (number) - The property ID as the key, with the numeric value you want to set. Can be an integer or decimal.

## Response Format

### Success Response (200 OK)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {}
}
```

- `success` (boolean) - Indicates whether the operation was successful
- `data` (object) - Contains additional response data (typically empty for successful updates)

## Prerequisites

⚠️ **Important:** Before running this request, ensure the following variables are set:

1. **`current_item_id`** - Must be set from a previous request (e.g., "Get Items")
2. **`prop_number`** - The ID of the number property you want to update

The pre-request script automatically validates these variables and will throw an error if they are missing.

## Pre-Request Script Validation

This request includes a pre-request script that:
- Retrieves the `current_item_id` and `prop_number` from collection variables
- Logs the values to the console for debugging
- Throws an error with message "❌ Brak ID! Uruchom najpierw 'Get Items'." if either variable is missing

## Example Usage

1. First, run a request to get items and set the `current_item_id` variable
2. Ensure the `prop_number` variable contains the ID of your number property
3. Modify the numeric value in the request body (e.g., change `42.5` to your desired number)
4. Send the request to update the property value

## Notes

- This endpoint is specifically for **number properties** only
- For other property types (text, link, people, etc.), use the corresponding "Update Property [Type]" requests
- The numeric value can be an integer or a decimal (floating-point number)
- The property ID must be valid and exist in the target item's database schema

- The property ID must correspond to a **People-type property** in your database

# Update Property Rating

## Overview
This endpoint updates a rating property value for a specific item in the database. It allows you to set a numerical rating (e.g., 1-5 stars) for an item's rating property field.

## Endpoint Details

**Method:** `PATCH`  
**URL:** `{{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values`

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of the API (e.g., `https://api.example.com`) |
| `{{current_item_id}}` | The unique identifier of the item whose rating property you want to update |

## Request Body

The request body uses a `cellValues` object structure where property IDs are mapped to their new values:

```json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_rating}}": 5
  }
}
```

### Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `cellValues` | Object | Container object for property updates |
| `{{prop_rating}}` | Number | The rating value to set (typically 1-5, depending on the property configuration) |

**Note:** The `{{prop_rating}}` variable represents the rating property ID and is dynamically resolved. If not already set in the environment, it will be automatically fetched by the pre-request script.

## Pre-Request Script Automation

This request includes an intelligent pre-request script that automatically handles the rating property ID:

- **Checks** if `prop_rating` environment variable exists
- **If missing**, fetches all properties from the database using the `database_id` environment variable
- **Searches** for a property with `type === 'rating'`
- **Sets** the `prop_rating` environment variable with the found property ID
- **Requires** `database_id` to be set in the environment for automatic fetching

This automation ensures the request works seamlessly without manual property ID lookup.

## Response Format

### Success Response (200 OK)

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "property": {
      "id": "property_id_here",
      "type": "rating",
      "options": {
        "max": 5,
        "icon": "star"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | Boolean | Indicates whether the operation was successful |
| `data` | Object | Contains the updated property information |
| `data.property` | Object | Details about the rating property |
| `data.property.id` | String | The unique identifier of the rating property |
| `data.property.type` | String | Property type (should be "rating") |
| `data.property.options` | Object | Configuration options for the rating property |
| `data.property.options.max` | Number | Maximum rating value allowed |
| `data.property.options.icon` | String | Icon type used for the rating display |

## Test Assertions

This request includes automated tests that validate:

1. **Status Code Validation**
   - Ensures the response returns HTTP 200 status code

2. **Success Flag Validation**
   - Verifies that `success` field in response is `true`

3. **Response Structure Validation**
   - Checks that response contains the `property` object
   - Validates that property type is "rating"
   - Verifies presence of `options.max` and `options.icon` fields

4. **Environment Variable Update**
   - If the response contains a property ID, it updates the `prop_rating` environment variable for future use

## Prerequisites

Before using this endpoint, ensure the following environment variables are set:

- `base_url` - Your API base URL
- `current_item_id` - The ID of the item to update
- `database_id` - Required for automatic property ID fetching
- `token` - Authentication token (used in pre-request script)
- `prop_rating` - (Optional) Rating property ID - will be auto-fetched if not set

## Example Usage

1. Set the required environment variables
2. Modify the rating value in the request body (default is 5)
3. Send the request
4. The pre-request script will automatically fetch the rating property ID if needed
5. The response will confirm the update and tests will validate the structure

# Update Property Comments

## Overview
This endpoint updates the comment cell values for a specific item in your Hypris database. It allows you to add or modify comments associated with an item by targeting the comments property field.

## Endpoint Details
- **Method**: `PATCH`
- **URL**: `{{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values`

## Variables Used

| Variable | Description | Example Value |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL for the Hypris API | Set in the active environment |
| `{{current_item_id}}` | The unique identifier of the item to update | Set in collection variables |
| `{{prop_comments}}` | The property ID for the comments field | `697c894445ec90247f1cb1b6` |

## Request Body Structure

The request body uses a dynamic structure where the property ID is used as a key:

```json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_comments}}": [
      {
        "text": "Pierwszy komentarz testowy"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Body Parameters
- **cellValues** (object): Container for all cell value updates
  - **{{prop_comments}}** (array): Array of comment objects for the comments property
    - **text** (string): The comment text content

## Pre-Request Script Behavior

The pre-request script automatically sets the `prop_comments` variable before the request is sent:

```javascript
pm.collectionVariables.set("prop_comments", "697c894445ec90247f1cb1b6");
```

This ensures the correct property ID is used for the comments field. The script also logs:
- ✅ The comments property ID
- 📍 The current item ID being updated

## Expected Response

### Success Response (200 OK)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    // Updated item data
  }
}
```

### Response Fields
- **success** (boolean): Indicates whether the operation was successful
- **data** (object): Contains the updated item information

## Post-Response Validation

The request includes automated tests that verify:
1. Status code is 200
2. Response contains `success: true` field (only checked if status is 200)

## Usage Notes

- The comments property accepts an array, allowing multiple comment entries
- The property ID (`prop_comments`) is hardcoded in the pre-request script for consistency
- Ensure the `current_item_id` variable is set before running this request
- The request will update existing comments or add new ones based on the API's merge behavior
- Check the console logs to verify the correct property and item IDs are being used

## Related Requests

This request is part of a series of property update endpoints. See also:
- Update Property Text
- Update Property People
- Update Property Link
- Update Property Location

# Upload File to Database Item Property

This endpoint uploads a file to a Files property of a database item. It accepts multipart/form-data and stores the file, returning comprehensive metadata about the uploaded file.

## Overview

Use this endpoint to attach files (images, documents, etc.) to a Files-type property within a database item. The uploaded file is stored and associated with the specified item and property.

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `current_item_id` | string | **Required.** The unique identifier of the database item to which the file will be uploaded |
| `prop_files` | string | **Required.** The unique identifier of the Files property where the file will be stored |

## Request Body

**Content-Type:** `multipart/form-data`

The request body should contain the file to be uploaded as form-data:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `file` | file | **Required.** The file to upload (supports various formats: images, documents, etc.) |

## Authentication

This endpoint requires authentication using the following credentials:

- `{{login}}` - User login/username
    
- `{{password}}` - User password
    

Ensure these variables are set in your environment before making the request.

## Response

### Success Response (200 OK)

Returns a JSON object with the uploaded file details:

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "cellFileItem": {
      "id": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "databaseItemId": "string",
      "databasePropertyId": "string",
      "file": {
        "url": "string",
        "size": number,
        "mimeType": "string",
        "commonFileType": "string",
        "variants": [],
        "id": "string",
        "originalName": "string"
      },
      "authorEntity": {
        "entityType": "user",
        "entityId": "string",
        "payload": null
      },
      "createdAt": "ISO 8601 timestamp",
      "updatedAt": "ISO 8601 timestamp"
    }
  }
}

 ```

### Response Fields

- `success` - Boolean indicating if the upload was successful
    
- `data.cellFileItem.id` - Unique identifier for the uploaded file item
    
- `data.cellFileItem.title` - Display title of the file
    
- `data.cellFileItem.databaseItemId` - ID of the parent database item
    
- `data.cellFileItem.databasePropertyId` - ID of the Files property
    
- `data.cellFileItem.file.url` - Direct URL to access the uploaded file
    
- `data.cellFileItem.file.size` - File size in bytes
    
- `data.cellFileItem.file.mimeType` - MIME type of the file (e.g., "image/jpeg", "application/pdf")
    
- `data.cellFileItem.file.commonFileType` - General file category (e.g., "image", "document")
    
- `data.cellFileItem.file.originalName` - Original filename as uploaded
    
- `data.cellFileItem.authorEntity` - Information about the user who uploaded the file
    
- `data.cellFileItem.createdAt` - Timestamp when the file was uploaded

request.description:
# Get Full Data Options for Status Property

## Overview
This endpoint retrieves the complete configuration and full data options for a specific status property. It returns all statuses associated with the property, including their IDs, titles, colors, positions, and related metadata.

## Purpose
Use this GET request to fetch the comprehensive configuration of status options for a particular property. This is essential for:
- Verifying status property configuration after creation or modification
- Retrieving all available status options for a property
- Checking the current state of statuses including their order and styling
- Validating that newly added statuses are properly configured

## URL Structure
```
GET {{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedStatusPropertyId}}/full-data-options
```

### Path Parameters
- **`{{base_url}}`** - The base URL of the API (e.g., `https://api.hypris.com`)
- **`{{newlyCreatedStatusPropertyId}}`** - The unique identifier of the status property for which you want to retrieve full data options

## Expected Response

### Success Response (200 OK)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "fullDataOptions": {
      "statuses": [
        {
          "id": "697b784d409d3115e48a16e2",
          "title": "Nazwa nowego statusu2",
          "color": {
            "colorType": "palette",
            "payload": 44
          },
          "databasePropertyId": "697b7840409d3115e48a169a",
          "position": 2
        }
      ],
      "isFilled": true
    }
  }
}
```

### Response Fields
- **`success`** (boolean) - Indicates whether the request was successful
- **`data.fullDataOptions`** (object) - Container for the full data options
  - **`statuses`** (array) - Array of status objects configured for this property
    - **`id`** (string) - Unique identifier of the status
    - **`title`** (string) - Display name of the status
    - **`color`** (object) - Color configuration for the status
      - **`colorType`** (string) - Type of color (e.g., "palette")
      - **`payload`** (number) - Color value or palette index
    - **`databasePropertyId`** (string) - ID of the parent database property
    - **`position`** (number) - Order position of the status in the list
  - **`isFilled`** (boolean) - Indicates whether the property has been populated with data

## Use Cases

### 1. Verify Status Creation
After creating a new status using the "Create Status" endpoint, call this endpoint to confirm the status was added successfully and retrieve its ID.

### 2. Audit Status Configuration
Use this endpoint to review all configured statuses for a property, including their colors, positions, and titles.

### 3. Retrieve Status IDs
When you need to reference specific statuses in other API calls, use this endpoint to get their unique IDs.

### 4. Check Property State
Verify whether a status property has been filled with data (`isFilled` flag) before performing operations that depend on it.

## Required Variables

| Variable | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `base_url` | Base URL of the Hypris API | `https://api.hypris.com` |
| `newlyCreatedStatusPropertyId` | ID of the status property | `697b7840409d3115e48a169a` |

## Example Workflow
1. Create a status property using the "Create Property Status" endpoint
2. Add statuses to the property using the "Create Status" endpoint
3. **Call this endpoint** to retrieve and verify all configured statuses
4. Use the retrieved status IDs in subsequent operations (e.g., filtering, updating)

## Notes
- This endpoint requires authentication (ensure your environment has valid `login` and `password` variables)
- The response includes all statuses in their configured order (sorted by `position`)
- The `isFilled` flag helps determine if the property is ready for use in database operations

request.description:
# Start Time Tracker for Item Property

## Overview

This endpoint starts a new time tracker entry for a specific property of a database item. It creates a time tracker record that begins tracking time immediately upon creation.

## URL Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of the Hypris API |
| `{{current_item_id}}` | The unique identifier of the database item for which to start time tracking |
| `{{prop_time_tracker}}` | The property ID of the time tracker field in the database |

## Request Body

``` json
{
  "shouldStartOnCreate": true
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `shouldStartOnCreate` | boolean | Yes | When set to `true`, the time tracker starts immediately upon creation. The `startedAt` timestamp will be set to the current time. |

## Response Format

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "databaseTimeTrackerItem": {
      "id": "string",
      "authorEntity": {
        "entityType": "user",
        "entityId": "string",
        "payload": {
          "id": "string",
          "nick": "string",
          "statusText": "string",
          "statusType": "string",
          "completedAvatarFileVariants": []
        }
      },
      "startedAt": "ISO 8601 timestamp",
      "endedAt": null,
      "databaseItemId": "string",
      "databasePropertyId": "string",
      "authorEntityId": "string",
      "databaseId": "string",
      "isStartEdited": false,
      "isEndEdited": false
    }
  }
}

 ```

### Response Fields

- **success**: Boolean indicating if the operation was successful
    
- **data.databaseTimeTrackerItem**: The created time tracker entry object
    - **id**: Unique identifier for this time tracker entry
        
    - **authorEntity**: Full details of the user who started the tracker
        
    - **startedAt**: ISO 8601 timestamp when tracking began
        
    - **endedAt**: Timestamp when tracking ended (null while active)
        
    - **databaseItemId**: Reference to the parent database item
        
    - **databasePropertyId**: Reference to the time tracker property
        
    - **databaseId**: Reference to the parent database
        
    - **isStartEdited**: Flag indicating if the start time was manually edited
        
    - **isEndEdited**: Flag indicating if the end time was manually edited
        

## Notes

- Only one active time tracker can run per user per item property at a time
    
- The `endedAt` field will be `null` until the time tracker is stopped
    
- The `authorEntity` contains complete user information including avatar variants
    
- Time tracker entries are associated with specific database items and properties
    
- The response includes flags (`isStartEdited`, `isEndEdited`) to track manual time adjustments

# End All Active Time-Tracker Items

## Overview

This endpoint stops all currently active time-tracker items for the authenticated user. It's useful when you need to ensure no time trackers are running, such as at the end of a work session or when switching contexts.

## Endpoint Details

- **Method:** `DELETE`
    
- **Path:** `{{base_url}}/me/active-time-tracker-items`
    
- **Full URL:** `{{base_url}}/me/active-time-tracker-items`
    

## Authentication

This endpoint requires authentication using the following variables:

- `{{base_url}}` - The base API URL for your Hypris instance
    
- `{{login}}` - Your login credentials
    
- `{{password}}` - Your password
    

Make sure these variables are configured in your active environment before making the request.

## Response Format

**Success Response (200 OK):**

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": null
}

 ```

The response indicates whether the operation was successful. The `data` field is `null` as this is a termination operation that doesn't return specific data.

## Important Notes

- This endpoint ends **all** active time-tracker items for the current user, not just a specific one
    
- If no time trackers are currently active, the endpoint will still return a success response
    
- The operation is idempotent - calling it multiple times has the same effect as calling it once
    
- Response time is typically under 800ms
    

## Use Cases

- End of workday cleanup to stop all running timers
    
- Switching between projects or tasks
    
- Ensuring clean state before starting new time tracking
    
- Automated workflows that need to reset time tracking state

# Update Time Tracker Item

## Overview
This endpoint updates an existing time tracker item with new start and end times. It allows you to modify the time tracking records for a specific item in your database.

## HTTP Method
`PATCH`

## URL Pattern
```
{{base_url}}/time-tracker-item/{{database_time_tracker_item_id}}
```

## Path Variables
| Variable | Type | Required | Description |
|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `database_time_tracker_item_id` | string | Yes | The unique identifier of the time tracker item to update |

## Request Body
The request body must be in JSON format with the following structure:

```json
{
  "startedAt": "2025-01-30T10:00:00.000Z",
  "endedAt": "2025-01-30T11:30:00.000Z"
}
```

### Body Fields
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `startedAt` | string | Yes | ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the time tracking started |
| `endedAt` | string | Yes | ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the time tracking ended |

**Note:** Both timestamps must be in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`)

## Expected Response
**Status Code:** `200 OK`

**Response Body:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": null
}
```

The response indicates successful update with a `success` field set to `true` and `data` field set to `null`.

1. **Status Code Validation** - Verifies the response status code is 200 OK
2. **JSON Format Validation** - Ensures the response is valid JSON
3. **Success Field Presence** - Confirms the response contains a 'success' field
4. **Success Value Validation** - Verifies 'success' is set to true
5. **Performance Check** - Ensures response time is below 800ms
6. **Method Verification** - Confirms the request method is PATCH
7. **Content-Type Header** - Validates Content-Type header is application/json
8. **Error Field Check** - Ensures no 'error' field exists in the response
9. **Response Size Check** - Verifies response size is reasonable (< 1KB)
10. **Column Name Validation** - Validates new column name if provided (optional check)

All tests must pass for the request to be considered successful.

# Rename Property

## Overview
This endpoint allows you to rename an existing property (column) in a Hypris database. Use this to update the display name of a property without affecting its underlying data or configuration.

## HTTP Method
**PATCH** - Partial update of a property resource

## Endpoint
```
PATCH {{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedCreatedAtPropertyId}}
```

## Required Parameters

### Path Variables
- `{{newlyCreatedCreatedAtPropertyId}}` - The unique identifier of the property you want to rename

### Environment Variables
- `{{base_url}}` - The base URL for the Hypris API

## Request Body
The request body must be a JSON object with the following structure:

```json
{
  "title": "New Property Name"
}
```

### Fields
- **title** (string, required) - The new name for the property. Must be:
  - A non-empty string
  - Less than 255 characters
  - Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace

## Expected Response

### Success Response (200 OK)
```json
{
  "success": true
}
```

### Response Fields
- **success** (boolean) - Indicates whether the rename operation completed successfully

## Automated Tests
This request includes 10 comprehensive post-response tests:

```json
}
```

This endpoint updates the phone number value for a specific property in an item. It allows you to set or modify the phone field of an item in your Hypris database.

```json
{
  "cellValues": {

  }
}
```

  - `+1234567890` (USA)

```json
{
  "success": true,

}
```

- Solution: Ensure the phone number includes the country code prefix (e.g., `+48`)

# Delete Phone Property

## Overview
This endpoint deletes a phone-type property from the system. Once deleted, the property and all its associated data will be permanently removed.

## Endpoint Details

**HTTP Method:** `DELETE`

**Endpoint Structure:** `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedPhonePropertyId}}`

## Path Variables

| Variable | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `base_url` | The base URL of the API | Yes |
| `newlyCreatedPhonePropertyId` | The unique identifier of the phone property to delete | Yes |

## Request Headers

```
Content-Type: application/json
```

## Request Body

The request body contains the property details in JSON format:

```json
{
  "type": "phone",
  "state": "published",
  "title": "Phone test"
}
```

### Body Fields

| Field | Type | Description | Required |
|-------|------|-------------|----------|
| `type` | string | The property type (must be "phone" for phone properties) | Yes |
| `state` | string | The current state of the property (e.g., "published") | Yes |
| `title` | string | The display title of the property | Yes |

## Response Codes

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `200 OK` | Property successfully deleted with response body |
| `204 No Content` | Property successfully deleted without response body |

## Success Response

The API may return either:
- **200 OK** with a JSON response body (e.g., `{"success": true}`)
- **204 No Content** with no response body

Both indicate successful deletion of the phone property.

## Post-Deletion Behavior

After successful deletion:
- The phone property is permanently removed from the system
- The `newlyCreatedPhonePropertyId` environment variable is automatically cleared
- Any data associated with this property is no longer accessible

## Scripts

### Pre-Request Script
This request includes a pre-request script that generates 25 number properties and stores them in the environment variable `properties`. This is used for bulk property creation testing.

### Post-Response Script
The post-response script performs the following validations:
- ✅ Verifies the response status is either 200 or 204
- ✅ If status is 200, validates the JSON response confirms successful deletion
- ✅ Clears the `newlyCreatedPhonePropertyId` environment variable after deletion
- ✅ Logs appropriate messages for debugging

## Notes

- Ensure the `newlyCreatedPhonePropertyId` variable contains a valid property ID before executing this request
- Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone
- The pre-request script creates test data that may not be directly related to this specific deletion operation

# Create Property Mail

## Overview
This endpoint creates a new **mail/email property** (column) in a specified database. The mail property type is designed for storing email addresses with built-in validation, making it ideal for contact information, user profiles, or any data structure requiring email fields.

---

## Request Details

**Method:** `POST`

**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/database/{{database_id_baza1}}/property`

---

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `base_url` | string | The base URL of your Hypris API instance (e.g., `https://api.hypris.com`) |
| `database_id_baza1` | string | The unique identifier of the database where the mail property will be created |

---

## Request Headers

```
Content-Type: application/json
```

---

## Request Body Schema

The request body must be a JSON object with the following fields:

```json
{
  "type": "mail",
  "state": "published",
  "title": "Mail test"
}
```

### Body Parameters

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | Must be set to `"mail"` to create an email property |
| `state` | string | Yes | The visibility state of the property. Use `"published"` to make it active and visible |
| `title` | string | Yes | The display name for the mail property column (e.g., "Email Address", "Contact Email") |

---

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "property": {
      "id": "prop_abc123xyz",
      "type": "mail",
      "title": "Mail test",
      "state": "published"
    }
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the property was created successfully |
| `data.property.id` | string | The unique identifier of the newly created mail property (automatically saved to environment variable `newlyCreatedMailPropertyId`) |
| `data.property.type` | string | Confirms the property type (should be `"mail"`) |

| `data.property.state` | string | The current state of the property |

---

## Example Use Case

**Scenario:** You're building a customer relationship management (CRM) system and need to add an email field to your contacts database.

2. Send a POST request with the body:
   ```json

     "title": "Customer Email"
   }
   ```
3. The API creates the email property and returns its ID
4. The property ID is automatically saved to the environment variable `newlyCreatedMailPropertyId` for use in subsequent requests

---

## Notes

- **Email Validation:** The mail property type typically includes automatic email format validation to ensure data integrity
- **Multiple Emails:** Depending on your API configuration, this field may support storing multiple email addresses per record
- **Environment Variables:** The post-response script automatically captures the newly created property ID and stores it as `newlyCreatedMailPropertyId` for easy reference in other requests
- **Property State:** Using `"published"` state makes the property immediately visible and usable in the database interface

---

## Automated Testing

This request includes automated tests that verify:
- ✅ Successful creation (HTTP 200/201 response)
- ✅ Response contains `success: true`
- ✅ Property type is correctly set to `"mail"`
- ✅ Property ID is successfully extracted and saved

```json
{

}
```

# Update Property Mail

## Overview

This endpoint updates the email/mail property value for a specific item in the database. It allows you to set or modify the email address stored in a mail-type property field.

## Endpoint Structure

```
PATCH {{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values

 ```

### Path Variables

| Variable | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of the API | Set in environment variables |
| `{{current_item_id}}` | The unique identifier of the item to update | Retrieved from previous requests (e.g., Get Items) |

## Request Body

The request body uses a `cellValues` object structure where property IDs are mapped to their new values:

``` json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_mail}}": "test@example.com"
  }
}

 ```

### Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cellValues` | Object | Container for property updates |
| `{{prop_mail}}` | String (Variable) | The ID of the mail property column to update |
| Email value | String | The email address to set (must be valid email format) |

## Pre-request Script Functionality

The pre-request script automatically configures the required variables before sending the request:

1. **Sets Property ID**: If `prop_mail` is not already set, it defaults to `697c890345ec90247f1caf81`
    
2. **Prepares Email Value**: Sets `email_value` to `test@example.com`

``` javascript
}
 ```

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
  "success": true,
  "data": {}
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |

| `success` | Boolean | Indicates whether the update was successful |
| `data` | Object | Additional response data (typically empty for updates) |

### Test 1: Status Code Validation

- **Validates**: The `success` field in the response is `true`
    
- **Error Handling**: Logs an error message if the server returns a non-200 status
    

## Usage Notes

- Ensure the `prop_mail` variable contains a valid mail property ID for your database
    
- The email value should follow standard email format validation
    
- If the request fails, verify that:
    - The item ID (`current_item_id`) exists and is accessible
        
    - The property ID (`prop_mail`) corresponds to a mail-type property
        
    - The email format is valid
        
- This endpoint is part of a series of property update requests for different data types

# Delete Mail Property

## Overview
This endpoint permanently deletes a mail-type property from your workspace and removes all stored email addresses associated with this property from all records. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone.

## Endpoint Details
- **HTTP Method:** `DELETE`
- **Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedMailPropertyId}}`

## Path Variables
| Variable | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `newlyCreatedMailPropertyId` | The unique identifier of the mail property to be deleted | Yes |

## Expected Response Codes
- **200 OK** - The mail property was successfully deleted and the response includes confirmation details
- **204 No Content** - The mail property was successfully deleted with no response body

## What Happens When Called
When this endpoint is executed:
1. The specified mail property is permanently removed from the system
2. All email addresses stored in this property across all records are deleted
3. The property configuration and metadata are removed
4. This action cannot be reversed

## Important Notes
⚠️ **Warning:** This is a permanent operation. Once a mail property is deleted:
- All email data stored in this property will be lost
- The property cannot be recovered
- Any automations or integrations using this property will be affected
- Consider backing up important data before deletion

## Usage Example
Make sure the `newlyCreatedMailPropertyId` variable is set in your environment before calling this endpoint. The property ID should correspond to an existing mail-type property in your workspace.

# Create Property Location

## Overview
This endpoint creates a new **location property** (field/column) in a specified database. Location properties can store various types of geographic information including street addresses, GPS coordinates, place names, and other location-based data.

---

## Request Details

### HTTP Method
`POST`

### Endpoint Structure
```
{{base_url}}/database/{{database_id_baza1}}/property
```

**Path Variables:**
- `base_url` - The base API URL for your Hypris instance
- `database_id_baza1` - The unique identifier of the database where the location property will be created

### Required Headers
```
Content-Type: application/json
```

---

## Request Body Parameters

The request body must be a JSON object with the following fields:

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | The property type. Must be set to `"location"` for location fields |
| `state` | string | Yes | The publication state of the property. Use `"published"` to make it immediately available |
| `title` | string | Yes | The display name/label for the location property field |

### Example Request Body
```json
{
  "type": "location",
  "state": "published",
  "title": "Location test"
}
```

---

## Response Format

### Success Response (200/201)
The API returns a JSON object containing the newly created property details:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "property": {
      "id": "property_id_here",
      "type": "location",
      "state": "published",
      "title": "Location test",
      ...
    }
  }
}
```

**Key Response Fields:**
- `success` - Boolean indicating if the operation was successful
- `data.property.id` - The unique identifier of the newly created location property
- `data.property.type` - Confirms the property type (should be "location")
- `data.property.title` - The title/name of the property
- `data.property.state` - The publication state

---

## Use Cases for Location Fields

Location properties are versatile and can be used for:

- **Street Addresses** - Store complete mailing addresses for contacts, offices, or delivery locations
- **GPS Coordinates** - Save latitude/longitude pairs for precise geographic positioning
- **Place Names** - Record city names, landmarks, or points of interest
- **Geographic Data** - Store any location-based information for mapping, routing, or spatial analysis
- **Multi-location Tracking** - Manage databases with location-dependent records (stores, warehouses, events)

---

## Configured Scripts

### Pre-request Script
This request includes a pre-request script that generates an array of 25 number properties and stores them in the environment variable `properties`. This is useful for batch property creation scenarios.

```javascript
const properties = Array.from({ length: 25 }, (_, i) => ({
  type: "number",
  state: "published",
  title: `Number ${i + 1}`
}));
pm.environment.set("properties", JSON.stringify(properties));
```

### Post-response Script
After a successful request, the post-response script automatically:
1. Extracts the newly created location property ID from the response
2. Saves it to the environment variable `newlyCreatedLocationPropertyId`
3. Validates that the property was created successfully with type "location"
4. Runs assertions to confirm the response status and structure

This automation enables seamless chaining with subsequent requests that need to reference the newly created location property.

---

## Notes
- Ensure the database exists and you have proper permissions before creating properties
- The property ID returned in the response can be used for further operations (updating, deleting, or referencing the property)
- Location fields support various formats - check your API documentation for specific format requirements

# Rename Property Location

## Overview
This endpoint updates the display name (title) of an existing location property while preserving all stored location data and relationships. Use this when you need to change how a location property appears to users without affecting the underlying data structure or any associated location entries.

## Request Details

**Method:** `PATCH`

**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedLocationPropertyId}}`

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `{{base_url}}` | string | The base URL of the Hypris API (e.g., `https://api.hypris.com/v1`) |
| `{{newlyCreatedLocationPropertyId}}` | string | The unique identifier of the location property you want to rename |

### Request Body

The request body should be a JSON object with the following structure:

```json
{
  "title": "New Location Name"
}
```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | string | Yes | The new display name for the location property. Should be descriptive and user-friendly. |

### Headers

- `Content-Type: application/json`
- Authentication headers (as configured in the collection)

## Response Format

### Success Response (200 OK)

```json
{
  "success": true
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the rename operation completed successfully |

### Error Responses

- **400 Bad Request:** Invalid request body or missing required fields
- **401 Unauthorized:** Authentication credentials are missing or invalid
- **404 Not Found:** Location property with the specified ID does not exist
- **500 Internal Server Error:** Server-side error occurred

## Example Use Case

**Scenario:** You have a location property originally named "Office Locations" but want to rename it to "Branch Offices" to better reflect its purpose.

**Request:**
```http
PATCH {{base_url}}/property/abc123xyz
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "title": "Branch Offices"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true
}
```

After this operation, the location property will display as "Branch Offices" throughout the application, but all existing location data, references, and relationships remain intact.

## Important Notes

- ⚠️ **Non-Destructive Operation:** This endpoint only updates the display name. All location data, coordinates, and relationships are preserved.
- 🔒 **Permissions Required:** Ensure you have appropriate permissions to modify properties in the workspace.
- 📝 **Title Validation:** The title should be between 1-254 characters and cannot be empty.
- 🔄 **Immediate Effect:** Changes take effect immediately and will be reflected across all views and references.
- 💡 **Best Practice:** Use descriptive, meaningful names that clearly indicate the purpose of the location property to other team members.

## Related Operations

- **Create Location Property:** Use POST `/property` to create a new location property
- **Delete Location Property:** Use DELETE `/property/{id}` to remove a location property
- **Get Location Property:** Use GET `/property/{id}` to retrieve property details

# Update Property Location

This endpoint updates the location property cell values for a specific item in the database. It allows you to set or modify location data including address, coordinates, and address components.

## Endpoint

```
PATCH {{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values

 ```

## URL Parameters

- **`{{base_url}}`** - The base URL of your Hypris API instance
    
- **`{{current_item_id}}`** - The unique identifier of the item whose location property you want to update
    

## Request Body Structure

The request body uses a `cellValues` object where the key is the location property ID (referenced as `{{prop_location}}`), and the value contains the location data:

``` json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "{{prop_location}}": {
      "address": "ul. Marszałkowska 1, 00-001 Warszawa",
      "displayAddress": "Marszałkowska 1, Warszawa",
      "coordinates": [21.0122, 52.2297],
      "addressComponents": {
        "country": "Poland",
        "state": null,
        "county": null,
        "commune": null,
        "city": "Warszawa",
        "street": "ul. Marszałkowska 1",
        "postalCode": "00-001"
      }
    }
  }
}

 ```

### Location Object Fields

- **`address`** (string) - The full formatted address including street, postal code, and city
    
- **`displayAddress`** (string) - A shortened, user-friendly version of the address for display purposes
    
- **`coordinates`** (array) - An array containing longitude and latitude `[longitude, latitude]` in decimal degrees
    
- **`addressComponents`** (object) - Detailed breakdown of the address into individual components:
    - **`country`** (string) - Country name
        
    - **`state`** (string|null) - State or province (if applicable)
        
    - **`county`** (string|null) - County or district (if applicable)
        
    - **`commune`** (string|null) - Commune or municipality (if applicable)
        
    - **`city`** (string) - City name
        
    - **`street`** (string) - Street name and number
        
    - **`postalCode`** (string) - Postal/ZIP code
        

## Response

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {}
}

 ```

- **`success`** (boolean) - Indicates whether the operation was successful
    
- **`data`** (object) - Contains additional response data (may be empty for successful updates)
    

## Pre-Request Script

This request includes an automated pre-request script that handles the location property ID:

- If the `{{prop_location}}` variable is not set in your environment, the script automatically fetches the location property ID from the database
    
- It retrieves all properties for the database specified in `{{database_id}}`
    
- It searches for a property with type `location` and sets the `{{prop_location}}` environment variable
    
- This ensures the request can execute successfully without manual property ID lookup
    

**Note:** Make sure `{{database_id}}` is set in your environment variables for the pre-request script to work correctly.

## Post-Deletion Cleanup

# Create Link Property

## Overview
This request creates a new **link property** (column) in a specified database. Link properties enable relationships between records across databases, allowing you to connect and reference data from different sources within your workspace.

## Endpoint
```
POST {{base_url}}/database/{{database_id_baza1}}/property
```

### Purpose
Creates a new property of type "link" in the target database, which can be used to establish connections between records in different databases or within the same database.

## URL Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `{{base_url}}` | The base URL of your Hypris API instance |
| `{{database_id_baza1}}` | The unique identifier of the database where the link property will be created |

## Request Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | The property type. Must be set to `"link"` for creating link properties |
| `state` | string | Yes | The publication state of the property. Use `"published"` to make it immediately available |
| `title` | string | Yes | The display name for the link property column |

### Example Request Body
```json
{
  "type": "link",
  "state": "published",
  "title": "Link test"
}
```

## Pre-Request Script

The pre-request script generates an array of 25 number properties and stores it in the environment variable `properties`. This is useful for batch operations or testing scenarios where multiple properties need to be created.

```javascript
const properties = Array.from({ length: 25 }, (_, i) => ({
  type: "number",
  state: "published",
  title: `Number ${i + 1}`
}));
pm.environment.set("properties", JSON.stringify(properties));
```

**Note:** This script appears to be preparation for bulk property creation but is not directly used by this specific request.

## Post-Response Script

The post-response script performs the following actions:

1. **Parses the JSON response** from the API
2. **Validates the response** to ensure the link property was successfully created
3. **Extracts the property ID** from the response (`responseJson.data.property.id`)
4. **Saves the ID** to the environment variable `newlyCreatedLinkPropertyId` for use in subsequent requests
5. **Runs automated tests** to verify:
   - The response status code is 200 or 201
   - The `success` field is `true`
   - The property type is correctly set to `"link"`

This automation enables chaining requests together, where the newly created link property ID can be referenced in follow-up operations.

## Expected Response Format

### Success Response (200/201)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "property": {
      "id": "prop_abc123xyz",
      "type": "link",
      "state": "published",
      "title": "Link test",
      "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    }
  }
}
```

### Error Response
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "message": "Error description",
    "code": "ERROR_CODE"
  }
}
```

## Use Cases

### 1. **Cross-Database Relationships**

## Overview
This endpoint modifies the name or label of an existing link property without affecting the stored URL data. Use this operation when you need to update how a link property is displayed or referenced, while preserving all existing link values associated with that property.

## Request Details

**Method:** `PATCH`

**Endpoint Pattern:** `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedLinkPropertyId}}`

## Required Variables

| Variable | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|

This endpoint requires authentication using the `login` and `password` variables configured in your environment.

## Request Body

The request body should be sent as JSON with the following structure:

```json
{
  "title": "New Property Name"
}
```

### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | string | Yes | The new name/label for the link property |

## Expected Response

**Success Response (200 OK):**

```json
{
  "success": true
}
```

### Response Fields

- `success` (boolean): Indicates whether the rename operation completed successfully

## Example Use Case

- Reorganize your property structure by removing obsolete link properties

## Notes

⚠️ **Warning**: This operation is irreversible. Once a property is deleted, it cannot be recovered. Ensure you have the correct property ID before executing this request.

# Create Property Relation

This endpoint creates a **relation property** (field) in a database that enables connecting records to other records, either within the same database or across different databases.

## What is a Relation Property?

A relation property is a special field type that establishes connections between records, similar to foreign keys in traditional databases. This enables:
- **Data linking**: Connect related information across your databases
- **Referential integrity**: Maintain consistent relationships between records
- **Bi-directional relationships**: When you create a relation, you can also create a reverse-relation property in the related database
- **Cross-database connections**: Link records from different databases within your workspace

## Request Body Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | Yes | Must be set to `"relation"` to create a relation property |
| `state` | string | Yes | The visibility state of the property. Use `"published"` to make it visible |
| `title` | string | Yes | The display name for the relation property/column |
| `relatedDatabaseId` | string | Conditional | The ID of the database you want to relate to. Required when creating a relation to another database |

### Example Request Body

```json
{
  "type": "relation",
  "state": "published",
  "title": "Related Items",
  "relatedDatabaseId": "database-uuid-here"
}
```

## Response Structure

The API returns a JSON response with the following structure:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "property": {
      "id": "property-uuid",
      "type": "relation",
      "title": "Relation test",
      "relatedDatabaseId": "related-database-uuid",
      // ... other property metadata
    }
  }
}
```

### Response Fields

- **success** (boolean): Indicates whether the property was created successfully
- **data.property.id** (string): The unique identifier for the newly created relation property
- **data.property.type** (string): Confirms the property type as "relation"
- **data.property.relatedDatabaseId** (string): The ID of the database this relation connects to
- **data.property.title** (string): The display name of the relation property

## Important Notes

### About relatedDatabaseId

- The `relatedDatabaseId` specifies which database the relation connects to
- If relating to the same database (self-referential), use the same database ID as in the URL
- If relating to a different database, provide that database's ID
- The related database must exist and be accessible in your workspace

### How Relationships Work

1. **Creating the Relation**: When you create a relation property, it adds a new column to your database that can store references to records in the related database
2. **Linking Records**: After creating the relation property, you can populate it by adding record IDs from the related database
3. **Reverse Relations**: Consider creating a corresponding reverse-relation property in the related database for bi-directional navigation
4. **Data Integrity**: The system maintains referential integrity, ensuring linked records remain valid

## Use Cases

- **Project Management**: Link tasks to projects, or team members to tasks
- **E-commerce**: Connect products to categories, orders to customers
- **Content Management**: Relate articles to authors, tags to posts
- **CRM**: Link contacts to companies, deals to contacts

# Rename Relationship Property

## Overview

This endpoint allows you to update/rename an existing relationship property in your database. The operation preserves all established connections between records, ensuring data integrity while allowing you to modify the property's display name.

## Request Details

**Method:** `PATCH`

**Endpoint:** `{{base_url}}/property/{{newlyCreatedRelationPropertyId}}`

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `newlyCreatedRelationPropertyId` | string | Yes | The unique identifier of the relationship property you want to rename |

### Request Body

The request body should be a JSON object with the following structure:

``` json
{
  "title": "New Property Name"
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `title` | string | Yes | The new name for the relationship property. Should be descriptive and meaningful for your use case |

### Headers

- `Content-Type: application/json`
    

## Response

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "success": true
}

 ```

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the rename operation completed successfully |

## Use Cases

- **Refactoring database schema**: Update property names to match new naming conventions or business terminology
    
- **Improving clarity**: Rename properties to make their purpose more obvious to team members
    
- **Localization**: Change property names to match different language requirements
    
- **Correcting typos**: Fix naming mistakes without losing existing data relationships
    

## Important Notes

⚠️ **Connection Preservation**: All existing relationships and connections between records are maintained during the rename operation. Only the display name of the property changes.

💡 **Best Practices**:

- Use clear, descriptive names that indicate the relationship type
    
- Follow your team's naming conventions
    
- Consider the impact on any external integrations that reference this property by name
    

🔒 **Permissions**: Ensure you have appropriate permissions to modify properties in the target database.

# Update Property Relation

## Overview

This endpoint updates relation property values for a specific item in Hypris. Relation properties allow you to link items across different databases, creating connections between related data.

## Endpoint

```
PATCH {{base_url}}/item/{{current_item_id}}/cell-values

 ```

### URL Structure

- **`{{base_url}}`** - The base URL for the Hypris API
    
- **`{{current_item_id}}`** - The unique identifier of the item whose relation property you want to update
    

## Authentication

This request requires authentication using the `login` and `password` variables configured in your environment.

## Request Body

The request body uses a `cellValues` object structure:

``` json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "<property_id>": ["<related_item_id_1>", "<related_item_id_2>"]
  }
}

 ```

### Body Parameters

- **`cellValues`** (object, required) - Container object for property updates
    
    - (string key) - The unique identifier of the relation property you want to update
        
        - Example: `697c88f345ec90247f1cae9d`
            
        - Replace this with the actual relation property ID from your database
            
    - **Value** (array of strings) - Array of item IDs from the related database that you want to link to this item
        
        - Each ID represents an item in the database that the relation property connects to
            

### Example

``` json
{
  "cellValues": {
    "697c88f345ec90247f1cae9d": ["697dd2c345ec90247f1ccda7"]
  }
}

 ```

## Response

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {}
}

 ```

The successful response confirms the relation property has been updated and returns:

- **`success`** - Boolean indicating the operation was successful
    
- **`data`** - Empty object for this endpoint
    

## Notes

- You can update multiple relation properties in a single request by adding more property ID keys to the `cellValues` object
    
- The related item IDs must exist in the database that the relation property is configured to connect to
    
- To find the correct property ID, use the database configuration endpoints or inspect existing items

# Delete Relation Property

## Overview
This endpoint permanently removes a relationship property from your workspace and breaks all connections between related records. Use this with caution as the operation cannot be undone.

## Endpoint
```
DELETE {{base_url}}/property/:propertyId
```

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `propertyId` | string | Yes | The unique identifier of the relation property to delete |

## What Happens
When you call this endpoint:
- The specified relationship property is permanently deleted
- All existing connections between records using this property are broken
- Related data associations are removed from the system
- The property becomes unavailable for future use

## Response Codes

| Status Code | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `200 OK` | Property successfully deleted with response body |
| `204 No Content` | Property successfully deleted without response body |
| `400 Bad Request` | Invalid property ID format |
| `404 Not Found` | Property ID does not exist |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Authentication required |
| `403 Forbidden` | Insufficient permissions to delete property |

## Prerequisites
- Valid authentication credentials
- The property ID of the relation property you want to delete (stored in `{{newlyCreatedRelationPropertyId}}` variable)
- Appropriate permissions to delete properties in the workspace

## Important Notes

⚠️ **Warning**: This operation is irreversible. Once a relation property is deleted:
- All relationship connections are permanently lost
- There is no way to recover the deleted property or its connections
- Any views, filters, or automations using this property may be affected

💡 **Best Practice**: Before deleting, ensure you have:
- Verified the correct property ID
- Confirmed that no critical workflows depend on this relationship
- Backed up any important data if necessary
